dhp334:2: his craving grows like a creeping vine.
dhp334:3: He runs now here & now there,
dhp334:4:
as if looking for fruit:
a monkey in the forest.
dhp335:3: your sorrows grow like wild grass
dhp336:1: If, in the world, you overcome
dhp336:2: this uncouth craving, hard to escape,
dhp336:4: like water beads off a lotus.
dhp337:1:
To all of you gathered here I say:
Good fortune.
dhp337:4: –as when seeking medicinal roots, wild grass– by the root.
dhp337:5: Don’t let Mara cut you down
dhp337:6: –as a raging river, a reed– over & over again.
dhp338:1: If its root remains undamaged & strong,
dhp338:2: a tree, even if cut, will grow back.
dhp338:3: So too if craving-obsession is not rooted out,
dhp339:2: flowing to what is appealing, are strong:
dhp339:4: carry him, of base views, away.
dhp340:1: They flow every which way, the streams,
dhp340:2: but the sprouted creeper stays in place.
dhp340:3: Now, seeing that the creeper’s arisen,
dhp340:4: cut through its root with discernment.
dhp341:2: are the joys of a person.
dhp341:3: People, bound by enticement, looking for ease:
dhp342:2: people hop round & around like a rabbit caught in a snare.
dhp342:4: they go on to suffering, again & again, for long.
dhp343:1: Encircled with craving, people hop round & around
dhp343:2: like a rabbit caught in a snare.
dhp343:3: So a monk should dispel craving,
dhp343:4: should aspire to dispassion for himself.
dhp344:1: Cleared of the underbrush but obsessed with the forest,
dhp344:2: set free from the forest, right back to the forest he runs.
dhp344:3: Come, see the person set free
dhp344:4: who runs right back to the same old chains!
dhp345:1: That’s not a strong bond –so say the enlightened–
dhp345:2: the one made of iron, of wood, or of grass.
dhp345:3: To be smitten, enthralled, with jewels & ornaments,
dhp345:4:
longing for children & wives:
that’s the strong bond, –so say the enlightened– one that’s constraining, elastic, hard to untie.
dhp346:3: But having cut it, they –the enlightened–go forth,
dhp346:4: free of longing, abandoning sensual ease.
dhp347:1: Those smitten with passion fall back into a self-made stream,
dhp347:2: like a spider snared in its web.
dhp347:3: But, having cut it, the enlightened set forth,
dhp347:4: free of longing, abandoning all suffering & stress.
dhp348:0: Gone to the beyond of becoming,
dhp348:1: you let go of in front, let go of behind,
dhp348:4: you don’t come again to birth & aging.
dhp349:1: For a person forced on by his thinking,
dhp349:2: fierce in his passion, focused on beauty,
dhp349:4: He’s the one who tightens the bond.
dhp350:1: But one who delights in the stilling of thinking,
dhp350:2: always mindful cultivating a focus on the foul:
dhp350:3: He’s the one who will make an end,
dhp350:4: the one who will cut Mara’s bond.
dhp351:1: Arrived at the finish, unfrightened, unblemished, free
dhp351:2: of craving, he has cut away
dhp351:4: This physical heap is his last.
dhp353:2: with regard to all things, unadhering.
dhp353:3: All-abandoning, released in the ending of craving:
dhp353:4: having fully known on my own, to whom should I point as my teacher?
dhp354:1: A gift of Dhamma conquers all gifts;
dhp354:2: the taste of Dhamma, all tastes;
dhp354:3: a delight in Dhamma, all delights;
dhp354:4: the ending of craving, all suffering & stress.
dhp355:1: Riches ruin the man weak in discernment,
dhp355:2: but not those who seek the beyond.
dhp355:3: Through craving for riches the man weak in discernment
dhp355:4: ruins himself as he would others.
dhp356:3: So what’s given to those free of passion
dhp357:3: So what’s given to those free of aversion
dhp358:3: So what’s given to those free of delusion
dhp359:3: So what’s given to those free of longing
